name: libperl5.40
title: libperl5.40
summary: cpan - easily interact with CPAN from the command line
description:
shared Perl library
This package contains the shared Perl library, used by applications
which embed a Perl interpreter.
.
It also contains the architecture-dependent parts of the standard
library (and depends on perl-modules-5.40 which contains the
architecture-independent parts).

commands.name: cpan5.40-x86_64-linux-gnu
commands.description: easily interact with CPAN from the command line

arguments.description: Creates a CPAN.pm autobundle with CPAN::Shell->autobundle.
arguments.raw: -a  Creates a CPAN.pm autobundle with CPAN::Shell->autobundle.

arguments.description: sensus.md>
Force the specified action, when it normally would have failed. Use this to install a mod-
arguments.raw: -f  Force the specified action, when it normally would have failed. Use this to install a mod-

arguments.description: Print a help message and exit. When you specify "-h", it ignores all of the other options
arguments.raw: -h  Print a help message and exit. When you specify "-h", it ignores all of the other options

arguments.description: Load "local::lib" (think like "-I" for loading lib paths). Too bad "-l" was already taken.
arguments.raw: -I  Load "local::lib" (think like "-I" for loading lib paths). Too bad "-l" was already taken.

arguments.description: List all installed modules with their versions
arguments.raw: -l  List all installed modules with their versions

arguments.description: Make the specified modules.
Do a dry run, but don't actually install anything. (unimplemented)
arguments.raw: -n  Do a dry run, but don't actually install anything. (unimplemented)

arguments.description: Show the out-of-date modules.
Find the best mirrors you could be using and use them for the current session.
arguments.raw: -P  Find the best mirrors you could be using and use them for the current session.

arguments.description: Ping the configured mirrors and print a report
arguments.raw: -p  Ping the configured mirrors and print a report

arguments.description: Recompiles dynamically loaded modules with CPAN::Shell->recompile.
arguments.raw: -r  Recompiles dynamically loaded modules with CPAN::Shell->recompile.

arguments.description: Drop in the CPAN.pm shell. This command does this automatically if you don't specify any
arguments.raw: -s  Drop in the CPAN.pm shell. This command does this automatically if you don't specify any

arguments.description: Do not test modules. Simply install them.
arguments.raw: -T  Do not test modules. Simply install them.

arguments.description: all installed modules. Blindly doing this can really break things, so keep a
arguments.raw: -u  all installed modules. Blindly doing this can really break things, so keep a

arguments.description: Print detailed information about the cpan client.
arguments.raw: -V  Print detailed information about the cpan client.

arguments.description: Print the script version and CPAN.pm version then exit.
arguments.raw: -v  Print the script version and CPAN.pm version then exit.

arguments.description: Turn on cpan warnings. This checks various things, like directory permissions, and tells
arguments.raw: -w  Turn on cpan warnings. This checks various things, like directory permissions, and tells

arguments.description: Dump all the namespaces to standard output.
arguments.raw: -X  Dump all the namespaces to standard output.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use cpan5.40-x86_64-linux-gnu because cpan - easily interact with CPAN from the command line.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use cpan5.40-x86_64-linux-gnu as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair cpan5.40-x86_64-linux-gnu with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `cpan5.40-x86_64-linux-gnu --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.readme: cpan - easily interact with CPAN from the command line
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install perl. cpan - easily interact with CPAN from the command line.
